title: No Taxpayer Funding for the World Health Organization Act
short_title: No Taxpayer Funding for the World Health Organization Act
stage_label: Referred to the House Committee on Foreign Affairs.
plain_summary: This bill would completely stop the United States from giving money to the World Health Organization. The US would not pay the membership fees that countries are required to pay. The US would also not give any extra money that it chooses to donate. This ban on funding would start right away when the bill becomes law.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Foreign Affairs.

sections.title: Section 1  Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the No Taxpayer Funding for the World Health Organization Act.

sections.title: Section 2  Prohibition on Assessed and Voluntary Contributions to the World Health Organization
sections.text: Starting on the day this law is passed, the United States cannot give any money to the World Health Organization. This includes both required payments that member countries must pay and optional donations that the United States chooses to give. No other law can override this rule.
